issues.sample: CAR HAS INTERMITTENT PROBLEM WITH ACCELERATION.   WILL GO INTO A FAIL SAFE MODE, WONT ACCELERATE PAST 40-60 MPH.  I HAVE HAD VERY CLOSE CALL ON MULTIPLE ACCIDENTS DUE TO THE FACT I AM UNABLE TO ACCELERATE.  MY CAR LOST POWER AND COULD NOT PICK UP SPEED AT ALL.   THIS IS A MAJOR S

I BOUGHT THE CAR IN MARCH 27 2018  THE  CAR WON'T NEVER CHARGE UP MY PHONE.  WHEN I USE MY CHARGER  TO CHARGE MY PHONE.  MY DAUGHTER HAS A GENESIS  AND IT DOES THE SAME THING AND HER CHARGE IN CAR BLEW OUT MY PHONE I'M SCARE THIS ELANTRA  GOING TO DO THE SAME IT WILL NOT CHARGE U

THE HEADLIGHTS BEAMS HARDLY REACH ACROSS AN INTERSECTION.. THEY DO NOT ILLUMINATE ROAD SIGNS LIKE SPEED LIMIT, AND STREET NAME SIGNS.. ITS IMPOSSIBLE TO DRIVE ON RURAL ROADS THAT HAVE DIPS IN THEM UNLESS YOUR SLOW DOWN TO 15MPH (NOT GOOD WHEN THE SPEED LIMIT IS 45 ON THESE ROADS.

narrative.headline: 2018 Hyundai Elantra: engine complaints dominate
narrative.summary: Owners filed 273 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is by far the most reported area at 49% (134 reports), followed by the electrical system at 15% and the power train at 9%. No deaths are reported. The engine reports include 7 fires and 3 crashes, the most of any area, so engine behavior deserves close attention on a test drive.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The dominant complaint by far: 134 reports (49%), including 7 fires, 3 crashes, and 1 injury. Watch for loss of power or failure to accelerate while driving.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 42 reports (15%), with 3 fires, 2 crashes, and 1 injury. The fire count makes this worth checking carefully.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 24 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. A recurring complaint area, often around shifting and acceleration.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 19 reports (7%), tied to 1 crash and 1 injury. A smaller complaint area, often involving hesitation during acceleration.

issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: 16 reports (6%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. The smallest of the top areas, mostly about weak headlight reach.
